claims.claim_text: A public model page does not guarantee unrestricted download; gated repositories can require account login, agreement to terms, and manual or automatic approval before files can be accessed.
claims.span_hint: section: Decoding the Illusion of Open, paragraphs on gated access
claims.rationale: The Hugging Face gated-models documentation directly describes request access, approval modes, authentication, and download requirements.

claims.claim_text: Model publishers often use custom licenses with materially different restrictions from standard open-source software licenses.
claims.span_hint: section: Decoding the Illusion of Open, paragraph on custom licenses
claims.anchor_text: Some licenses allow commercial use with attribution and policy restrictions. Others permit research only, require a separate agreement above a usage threshold, forbid using outputs to train competing models, or restrict deployment in regulated domains.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Publishers often use custom licenses with terms that differ materially from standard open-source software licenses.
claims.rationale: This sentence bundles several license-specific subclauses that are true only for some models and needs to be generalized unless each example is sourced individually.

claims.claim_text: Commercial-use review may apply to internal business workflows as well as directly sold AI features.
claims.span_hint: section: Decoding the Illusion of Open, paragraph on commercial use
claims.anchor_text: If it supports a private support assistant, helps employees triage customer tickets, drafts product documentation, ranks sales leads, or summarizes contracts for a revenue-generating company, the work may still fall under commercial use.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Commercial-use terms can apply to internal business workflows as well as directly sold products, so teams should review the license before operational use.
claims.rationale: The underlying lesson is sound, but the listed examples assert a broad legal conclusion that varies by license and should be stated more carefully.

claims.claim_text: A model card should document intended use, out-of-scope use, limitations, training data, evaluation details, and hardware or software requirements.
claims.span_hint: section: Working Through a Model Card, opening and later review paragraphs
claims.rationale: The annotated model-card template explicitly enumerates these sections and their purpose.

claims.claim_text: Instruction-tuned chat models expect model-specific chat formatting or control tokens, and using the wrong template can significantly degrade performance.
claims.span_hint: section: Working Through a Model Card, tokenizer configuration paragraph
claims.rationale: The Transformers chat-templating docs directly explain that different models use different control tokens and that wrong formatting causes much worse performance.

claims.claim_text: Safetensors was designed as a safer alternative to pickle-based model serialization because pickle formats can carry arbitrary code execution risk.
claims.span_hint: section: Reading a Model Hub Like a Deployment Registry, paragraph on Safetensors
claims.rationale: Hugging Face's security documentation directly states the arbitrary-code-execution risk of pickle files and frames Safetensors as the safer alternative.

claims.claim_text: Serving memory requirements can exceed raw weight size because active context and caches increase runtime memory use.
claims.span_hint: section: Working Through a Model Card, hardware sizing paragraph
claims.anchor_text: A seven or eight billion parameter model in half precision can require far more memory during real serving than the raw weights suggest, especially when multiple concurrent requests hold long contexts in memory.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Even mid-sized models can require substantially more memory during serving than raw weight size alone suggests, especially when long contexts and concurrent requests increase cache pressure.
claims.rationale: The exact parameter-count example is configuration-dependent and should be generalized unless it is tied to a specific published benchmark or serving setup.

claims.claim_text: Base causal language models are pretrained next-token predictors, while instruct or chat variants receive additional tuning for instruction-following behavior.
claims.span_hint: section: Choosing Among Base, Instruction Tuned, and Quantized Variants, opening paragraphs
claims.rationale: The chat-templating docs explicitly describe pretraining as creating a base model and later fine-tuning for chat.

claims.claim_text: Quantization reduces memory and compute costs by storing weights in lower precision such as 8-bit or 4-bit formats, often making larger models fit on constrained hardware.
claims.span_hint: section: Choosing Among Base, Instruction Tuned, and Quantized Variants, paragraph on quantization
claims.rationale: The Transformers quantization overview directly states that quantization lowers memory requirements by using lower-precision weights.

claims.claim_text: GGUF is a model file format for GGML-based executors that is designed for fast loading and single-file deployment in local inference workflows.
claims.span_hint: section: Choosing Among Base, Instruction Tuned, and Quantized Variants, paragraph on GGUF
claims.rationale: The GGUF specification directly states that the format is for inference with GGML executors and highlights fast loading and single-file deployment.

claims.claim_text: A LoRA adapter is loaded on top of a pretrained base model rather than replacing it with a standalone full model.
claims.span_hint: section: Choosing Among Base, Instruction Tuned, and Quantized Variants, paragraph on fine-tunes and adapters
claims.rationale: The PEFT LoRA guide shows adapters being loaded into a base model and discusses unloading or switching adapters while retaining the base.

claims.claim_text: Mistral is positioned as a model family that balances performance with efficient inference, using attention techniques that reduce memory requirements.
claims.span_hint: section: Evaluating Major Model Families, paragraph on Mistral
claims.rationale: The official Transformers Mistral page explicitly frames the model around efficient inference and reduced memory use.

claims.claim_text: Qwen's official materials describe multilingual pretraining and also publish coding-oriented model lines such as CodeQwen and Qwen-Coder.
claims.span_hint: section: Evaluating Major Model Families, paragraph on Qwen
claims.rationale: The official Qwen repository README discusses multilingual data and coding capabilities directly.

claims.claim_text: Gemma is a lightweight open-model family from Google with pretrained and instruction-tuned variants and is described as suitable for limited-resource environments.
claims.span_hint: section: Evaluating Major Model Families, paragraph on Gemma
claims.rationale: The official Gemma Hugging Face model card directly states these characteristics.

claims.claim_text: Longer active contexts increase inference memory pressure because the key-value cache grows and can become a bottleneck for long-context generation.
claims.span_hint: section: Evaluating Major Model Families, paragraph on context window size
claims.rationale: The KV-cache documentation explicitly says the cache can occupy a significant portion of memory and become a bottleneck for long-context generation.

claims.claim_text: A context window corresponds to the maximum input length a model can accept in a given workflow, and overlong inputs are typically truncated unless the application handles them another way.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know, bullet on context window
claims.rationale: The padding-and-truncation docs directly describe truncating inputs to the maximum length a model can accept.

further_reading.title: Annotated Model Card Template
further_reading.why_relevant: Best primary reference for what engineers should extract from a model card during intake.

further_reading.title: Hugging Face Gated Models
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers access requests, approval modes, authentication, and operational implications of gated model downloads.

further_reading.title: Transformers Quantization Overview
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful background for comparing full-precision, 8-bit, and 4-bit artifacts in local inference planning.

further_reading.title: GGUF Specification
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for what GGUF stores and why it is common in local GGML-based inference stacks.

notes: Excluded quiz prompts, hands-on instructions, diagrams, and the sources list itself per brief. `source_ids` are local placeholders paired with each claim's `proposed_url` because no shared section pool was selected.
